audit_status.source_base: Hebrew 1 Samuel 10, first-pass restored draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: Reviewed for anointing and kiss, sign sequence, prophetic-band altered state, identity-change language, Gilgal waiting instruction, private secrecy, public lot, hiding among baggage, height rhetoric, acclamation, royal custom, dissent, and mental-health/leader-cult safeguards.

verses.restored: Samuel took flask of oil, poured it upon Saul's head, kissed him, and said, Has not YHWH anointed you as leader over his inheritance?

When you go from me today, you will find two men by Rachel's tomb in border of Benjamin at Zelzah. They will say to you, Donkeys you went to seek are found, and look, your father has left matter of donkeys and is anxious for you, saying, What shall I do about my son?

You shall pass on from there and come to oak of Tabor. Three men going up to Divine at Bethel will meet you, one carrying three young goats, one carrying three loaves of bread, and one carrying a skin of wine.
They will ask about your well-being and give you two loaves of bread, and you shall take them from their hand.

Afterward you shall come to hill of Divine where Philistine garrison is. As you come there to city, you will meet a band of prophets coming down from high place, with harp, tambourine, flute, and lyre before them, and they will be prophesying.
Spirit of YHWH will rush upon you, and you will prophesy with them and be turned into another man.
When these signs come to you, do for yourself what your hand finds, for Divine is with you.

You shall go down before me to Gilgal, and look, I will come down to you to offer ascending offerings and sacrifice peace offerings. Seven days you shall wait until I come to you and make known to you what you shall do.
It happened as he turned his shoulder to go from Samuel, Divine turned for him another heart, and all these signs came that day.
They came there to hill, and look, a band of prophets met him. Spirit of Divine rushed upon him, and he prophesied among them.
It happened, when all who knew him before saw, and look, he prophesied with prophets, people said each to companion, What is this that has happened to son of Kish? Is Saul also among prophets?
A man from there answered and said, And who is their father? Therefore it became a proverb, Is Saul also among prophets?
When he finished prophesying, he came to high place.
Saul's uncle said to him and to his servant, Where did you go? He said, To seek donkeys; when we saw they were not found, we went to Samuel.
Saul's uncle said, Tell me, please, what Samuel said to you.
Saul said to his uncle, He plainly told us donkeys had been found. But matter of kingship, of which Samuel had spoken, he did not tell him.
Samuel called people together to YHWH at Mizpah.
He said to children of Israel, Thus says YHWH, Divine of Israel: I brought Israel up from Egypt, and I rescued you from hand of Egypt and from hand of all kingdoms oppressing you.

But today you have rejected your Divine, who saves you from all your evils and distresses, and you have said, No, but set a king over us. Now present yourselves before YHWH by tribes and thousands/clans.
Samuel brought near all tribes of Israel, and tribe of Benjamin was taken.
He brought near tribe of Benjamin by its clans, and clan of Matrites was taken; then Saul son of Kish was taken. They sought him, but he was not found.
They inquired again of YHWH, Has another man come here? YHWH said, Look, he has hidden himself among baggage.
They ran and took him from there. He stood among people, and he was taller than all people from his shoulders upward.
Samuel said to all people, Have you seen whom YHWH has chosen? There is none like him among all people. All people shouted and said, Let king live!
Samuel spoke to people judgment/custom of kingship, wrote it in book, and placed it before YHWH. Samuel sent all people away, each to house.
Saul also went to his house at Gibeah, and the force/valiant ones whose hearts Divine had touched went with him.
But worthless men said, How will this one save us? They despised him and brought him no gift. But he was as one silent.

translation_notes: The private anointing, public lot, prophetic experience, and popular acclamation are distinct legitimating scenes; none removes the need for accountability.
Samuel's kiss is an ancient greeting/commission gesture, not permission for leaders to cross bodily boundaries.
Prophetic music and altered behavior are communal religious phenomena, not diagnostic evidence of mental illness or automatic proof of holiness.
'Turned into another man' and 'another heart' describe vocational transformation; they must not erase identity, consent, or responsibility.
The instruction to do what the hand finds is limited by the surrounding commission and cannot authorize impulsive or violent behavior.
Saul hides among baggage. Fear, reluctance, overwhelm, or privacy do not disqualify a person or prove false humility.
Public selection by lot reflects ancient discernment and cannot replace transparent, participatory governance.
Saul's height again attracts attention; bodily traits are not leadership qualifications.
'Let the king live' is political acclamation, not worship.
The 'judgment/custom of kingship' should be read beside chapter 8's warning about royal extraction.
The dissenters' insult does not justify suppression, retaliation, or compulsory gifts; Saul's silence initially restrains escalation.
safety_note: Do not use anointing, prophecy, altered states, lots, bodily appearance, crowd acclaim, or claims that Divine touched supporters' hearts to demand political obedience. Do not stigmatize Saul's hiding or prophetic behavior as mental illness. Dissent is not treason, and leaders may not punish people for withholding praise or gifts.
